executive_briefing.summary: Liberation Day tariff shock activates Cascade Protocol; global stress regime escalates to CRITICAL; EM Equities collapse to BEARISH; Bonds, Metals flip BEARISH — 6 of 8 asset classes now BEARISH
executive_briefing.one_number_to_watch: CME futures: 60% probability no Fed cuts through end-2026. If that number falls below 40% (cut expectations return), it means the market has priced in a hard landing and expects emergency easing — the Fast Cascade scenario is underway.
executive_briefing.one_indicator_lying: Earnings revisions (+12.5% Q1 est.) remain GREEN with Blind Spot Rule 1 active. These figures pre-date tariff impact on corporate guidance. Q1 earnings season begins this week — watch for downside guidance from export-exposed sectors as the first real read-through of Liberation Day.

flags.title: Hyperscaler capex trajectory reinforces AI Infrastructure Debt indicator
flags.body: MAINTAINED: OpenAI $122B total funding at $852B valuation. Tariff shock applies directly to semiconductor supply chains and AI hardware imports — tariff cost on AI infrastructure capex is now a confirmed headwind. Data centre electricity cost inflation from energy shock erodes AI capex ROI. Private credit gate events undermine the cheap capital assumption behind AI capex funding models. AI capex remains both a debt amplifier (2026-2027 risk) and potential deflationary force (2028-2030).

flags.title: AI labour displacement beginning to show in entry-level employment data
flags.body: MAINTAINED: NBER Working Paper 34836: 502,000 AI-driven job cuts projected in 2026 — 9x the 55,000 in 2025. Critical dynamic: if realised, would push jobless_claims and consumer_confidence both RED, accelerating Consumer Staples BEARISH score. Fed cannot model AI displacement masked beneath aggregate labour statistics.

flags.title: EU CSRD Phase 2 creates mandatory climate risk disclosure for financial institutions
flags.body: MAINTAINED: CSRD Phase 2 in force since 1 January 2026; first reporting obligations 2027. Double materiality requirements expose EU financial institutions. Structural cost amplifier for G-SIB institutions already under capital pressure. Liberation Day tariffs add a parallel structural cost via supply chain compliance requirements.

flags.title: Iran/Hormuz conflict drives largest oil supply disruption in history — directly drives oil_supply_shock RED escalation
flags.body: MAINTAINED: Hormuz flows at ~4M bpd (vs normal 16M bpd). No AIS-confirmed commercial resumption. 5yr breakeven inflation +26bps since conflict onset — the energy shock is now directly embedded in CB policy. Sudan economic warfare and EM sovereign distress compounding. This is the dominant near-term driver of the GMM Energy score (-0.865 BEARISH) and key input into Consumer Staples and EM Equities.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: No FIMI-driven market manipulation identified. Hungary election resolved April 12 — outcome now known (watch for HUF and Hungarian sovereign spread reaction to post-election government formation).

flags.title: Hungary election April 12 — HUF and sovereign spread stress potential; tariff coercion as WDM-GMM structural link
flags.body: UPDATED: Hungary election April 12 is 5 days away. Tisza-Fidesz race remains tight. Macro relevance: Tisza victory + Russian economic retaliation scenario could spike HUF and Hungarian sovereign spreads. Additional WDM-GMM structural link: Liberation Day tariff package is politically driven economic coercion — WDM cms-005 flag (economic coercion as democratic erosion mechanism) is now confirmed at institutional level.

flags.title: US tariff shock (EU 20%) directly targets European economies — upgraded from NoSignal to Active
flags.body: UPGRADED: Liberation Day tariffs at 20% on EU are a direct economic coercion event — ESA cross-monitor relevance confirmed. ECB citing material near-term inflation from energy shock; EU Anti-Coercion Instrument filings initiated. Transatlantic decoupling protocol activated. Financial contagion from Treasury basis trade dislocation would affect ECB collateral availability. US-EU trade relationship is in structural transition — ESA designation upgraded from NoSignal to Active.

weekly_brief:
## Lead Signal
The EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in a 15 percent rate on EU exports reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness as tariffs on rivals like China converge to similar levels. EU export growth to the US is projected to slow by 4.6 percent in 2026 due to higher effective rates and relative disadvantage. This shifts trade friction from episodic uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss under the Tariff Shock Cascade Protocol. The deal confirms the US trade policy regime as entrenched structural policy per USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda with no de-escalation in sight. Three Strategic Anchor indicators corroborate this CRISIS regime: USTR agenda SA indicator confirms structural tariff entrenchment alongside EU-Mercosur agreement SA and Section 232 tariffs SA.

## Other Developments
**Tariff-driven inflation expectations** Tariff-driven inflation expectations affect rate path with breakevens at 26bps cited by Fed and ECB. This structural inflation component complicates central bank policy with Fed holding rates steady and ECB cutting 25bps amid divergent inflation trajectories. The Turnberry deal locks in tariff rates as persistent input cost pressure.

**EU-Mercosur trade realignment** EU splits Mercosur pact into EMPA and ITA signed January 16 2026 amid US tariff pressures signaling EU diversification from US trade reliance. This regime-level trade realignment impacts EM equities via Latin America exposure and metals/commodities flows confirming structural decoupling.

**Hormuz oil shock persistence** Unresolved Hormuz tensions drive Brent crude to 98 dollars creating tail risk for energy inflation. The oil supply shock remains a critical amplifier with breakevens at 26bps and no resolution in sight. Energy asset class registers BEARISH outlook with equity vs commodity divergence blind spot active.

**CRE delinquency surge** Office CMBS delinquency rate reaches 12.5 percent driven by remote work trends and tariff input costs. This triggers Private Credit redemption gate tail risk with contagion potential to commercial real estate and NBFI sectors.

## Cross-Monitor Connections
The Turnberry deal and EU Parliament vote signal entrenched US-EU economic coercion triggering ESA HIGH urgency flag on fiscal autonomy push. EU-Mercosur agreement advances trade realignment amid US tariff stress triggering SCEM MEDIUM urgency flag on EM sovereign stress. Section 232 steel aluminum persistence drives metals input inflation chain stress triggering ERM MEDIUM urgency flag on commodity markets.

## Outlook
Watch ECB forward guidance on tariff passthrough at May 7 meeting and USTR retaliation updates on USMCA review progress. Monitor EPFR EM flows for capital outflow signals and Q1 earnings for guidance revisions in exposed sectors. Hormuz shipping data remains critical for oil supply shock resolution with Brent crude above 95 dollars signaling persistent energy inflation risk. The CRISIS regime remains stable with VERY HIGH conviction as no Strategic Anchor indicators shift despite de-escalation rhetoric.

regime_audit.methodology_note: Published stress_regime is the synthesiser's stress_regime_preliminary (2-domain corroboration rule). The reasoner's recommended_regime uses a stricter 3-indicator SA corroboration test and is preserved here for audit. Divergence indicates a tactical shock the synth has priced in ahead of reasoner's structural confirmation. Methodology unification under SCOPE-2026-04-17-005.
